title: Ensure thatô Serviceô Accountô Tokensô areô onlyô mountedô whereô necessary

input_row.title: Ensure thatô Serviceô Accountô Tokensô areô onlyô mountedô whereô necessary
input_row.description: Service accounts tokens should not be mounted in pods except where the workload running in the pod explicitly needs to communicate with the API server
input_row.rationale: Mounting service account tokens inside pods can provide an avenue for privilege escalation attacks where an attacker is able to compromise a single pod in the cluster. Avoiding mounting these tokens removes this attack avenue.
input_row.audit: Review pod and service account objects in the cluster and ensure that the option below is set, unless the resource explicitly requires this access. automountServiceAccountToken: false
input_row.remediation: Modify the definition of pods and service accounts which do not need to mount service account tokens to disable it.
input_row.impact: Pods mounted without service account tokens will not be able to communicate with the API server, except where the resource is available to unauthenticated principals.
input_row.default_value: By default, all pods get a service account token mounted in them.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_service_account_tokens_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to list all pods and their associated service accounts.
- Execute the command: `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o=jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.spec.serviceAccountName}{"\t"}{.spec.automountServiceAccountToken}{"\n"}{end}'` to retrieve the names of all pods, their service account names, and the `automountServiceAccountToken` setting.
- Validate that the `automountServiceAccountToken` field is set to `false` for pods that do not require access to the Kubernetes API.
- The expected configuration for compliance is that `automountServiceAccountToken` should be `false` unless explicitly required by the workload.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: All pods that do not require API access have `automountServiceAccountToken` set to `false`.
  - Fail: Any pod that does not require API access has `automountServiceAccountToken` set to `true`.
